name: ESPresense node (ESP32/ESP32-C3, single-room)
modality: room-level BLE presence via RSSI trilateration, open-source firmware
privacy_note: Fully local MQTT publish only, no cloud; requires ESPresense firmware flash and a local MQTT broker.
notes: ~$5-10/node (generic ESP32 board); one node gives coarse room presence, 3+ nodes enable trilateration-grade room accuracy (see whole-home mesh variant).

name: Nordic nRF52840 Dongle as iBeacon/Eddystone transmitter
modality: Programmable BLE beacon transmitter for tagging people/objects
privacy_note: Local BLE broadcast only; no cloud dependency; MAC/UUID mapped locally to identity.
notes: ~10 EUR; needs custom firmware (nRF Connect SDK) to broadcast iBeacon frames; paired with a BLE scanner node for room mapping.

name: Generic ESP32 BLE scanner (passive advertisement sniffer)
modality: Passive BLE advertisement scanning (RSSI + MAC) for known-device presence
privacy_note: Local only; known-MAC allowlist processed on-box; no raw MAC data leaves home.
notes: ~4-6 EUR board; simplest DIY entry point, one scanner = coarse room-presence for known phones/tags.

name: Tile Mate (BLE finder tag)
modality: BLE tag broadcast for item/keychain tracking
privacy_note: Detectable via local BLE scan of its advertisement MAC/UUID; Tile's own app/cloud network is NOT used by Wavr, only the raw local BLE broadcast.
notes: ~25-30 EUR; primarily consumer finder tag, works as passive presence tag via local BLE scanner.

name: Apple AirTag
modality: BLE (+ U1 UWB with iPhone) precision finding tag
privacy_note: CAVEAT: AirTag rotates its BLE MAC address (privacy-by-design, anti-stalking), so persistent local identity mapping is unreliable without Apple's Find My network/cloud, which Wavr does not use. UWB ranging (via U1) requires an iPhone and Apple's Nearby Interaction framework -- not natively accessible to Wavr. Usable only for coarse, unreliable BLE presence detection.
notes: ~35 EUR; best avoided for reliable presence -- MAC rotation breaks simple allowlist tracking; UWB path is roadmap only.

name: iPhone (any modern model) as BLE presence device
modality: Smartphone BLE advertisement / randomized MAC presence
privacy_note: CAVEAT: iOS randomizes BLE MAC by default, weakening long-term identity matching; more reliable when the phone runs a companion BLE beacon app (e.g. a dedicated iBeacon-broadcasting app) with a fixed UUID. Native Wi-Fi CSI / U1 UWB from iPhone is NOT accessible to Wavr (roadmap).
notes: No hardware cost; identity best achieved by installing an iBeacon broadcaster app rather than relying on ambient BLE.

name: Android smartphone as BLE presence device
modality: Smartphone BLE advertisement + optional beacon-app broadcast
privacy_note: Local BLE scan only; some Android versions also randomize MAC, so a dedicated beacon-broadcast app with fixed identifier is recommended for stable presence mapping.
notes: No hardware cost; can also expose network-scan presence via Wi-Fi (see network-scan ingress in other categories).

name: Apple Watch (any Series) as BLE wearable presence
modality: Wearable BLE presence, correlates with paired iPhone owner identity
privacy_note: Same MAC-randomization caveat as iPhone; local BLE scan only, no Apple cloud involved.
notes: Useful as a secondary confirmation signal for a known person alongside their phone.

name: Wear OS smartwatch as BLE wearable presence
modality: Wearable BLE presence signal
privacy_note: Local BLE scan of advertisement; no cloud dependency for Wavr's use.
notes: Similar role to Apple Watch entry; secondary presence confirmation device.

name: KKmoon/KKM K9 iBeacon Bluetooth Beacon
modality: Battery-powered iBeacon transmitter, fixed UUID/major/minor
privacy_note: Fixed non-rotating identifier broadcast locally; no cloud, no pairing required.
notes: ~10-15 EUR; coin-cell powered, months-year battery life; good for room/zone anchors.

name: Minew E8 Beacon (iBeacon/Eddystone)
modality: Dual-mode iBeacon/Eddystone-URL/UID beacon with configurable interval/power
privacy_note: Local BLE broadcast only; configurable via Minew app over BLE (no persistent cloud link needed for operation).
notes: ~15-20 EUR; widely used commercial-grade beacon, replaceable battery, good range (~50-70m).

name: Estimote Proximity Beacon
modality: iBeacon/Eddystone beacon with temperature/motion sensors
privacy_note: Local broadcast usable without Estimote Cloud; Wavr ignores Estimote's own cloud analytics feature.
notes: ~30 EUR; discontinued line but many units still in circulation on secondary market.

name: ESP32-C3 SuperMini as low-cost wearable BLE tag
modality: DIY coin-sized BLE beacon tag for keychain/wearable identity tagging
privacy_note: Fully local; custom firmware means fixed non-rotating ID under admin's control (privacy-friendlier than commercial trackers with cloud networks).
notes: ~3-5 EUR board; needs battery + 3D-printed/case DIY assembly; best price/control tradeoff for admins comfortable flashing firmware.

name: ESPresense multi-node mesh (3-6x ESP32 nodes, whole-home deployment)
modality: Whole-home room-level trilateration mesh for BLE tags/phones
privacy_note: All processing/MQTT local; this is the most capable BLE presence deployment Wavr can ingest today without cloud egress.
notes: ~30-50 EUR total for a small home (5-6 nodes); best BLE-based room-accuracy achievable pre-UWB; feeds Wavr via MQTT topics per node.

name: Govee H5074 Bluetooth Thermo-Hygrometer (BLE beacon side-channel)
modality: Environmental sensor BLE advertisement, incidental fixed-MAC presence anchor
privacy_note: Local BLE advertisement scan only; Govee cloud app not required for Wavr's ingestion.
notes: ~10-12 EUR; dual-purpose device -- primarily temp/humidity sensor but its steady BLE broadcast doubles as a fixed room anchor for RSSI calibration.

name: Xiaomi Mi Band (any recent model) as BLE wearable presence
modality: Fitness band BLE advertisement presence signal
privacy_note: Local BLE scan of advertisement; some firmware versions randomize MAC periodically, reducing long-term reliability.
notes: ~30-40 EUR; inexpensive wearable option for identity-linked presence, secondary signal only.

name: Apple U1 UWB chip (iPhone 11+/Apple Watch Series 6+) Nearby Interaction
modality: Centimeter-accurate UWB ranging and direction between Apple devices
privacy_note: Apple's Nearby Interaction framework restricts UWB ranging to authorized companion apps on Apple platforms only; no public local API exists for a third-party hub like Wavr to passively range an iPhone's U1 chip today.
notes: Would require a custom iOS companion app using NearbyInteraction framework to ever bridge into Wavr; not a plug-and-play sensor.

name: Qorvo DWM3000 UWB Module (IEEE 802.15.4z)
modality: Standalone UWB transceiver module for precise ranging/positioning
privacy_note: Fully local hardware, no cloud -- but Wavr has no native UWB stack/driver integration yet; would need a custom firmware+ranging-anchor bridge (e.g. MQTT) to become usable.
notes: ~25-40 EUR/module; developer kit (DWM3000EVB) available; realistic DIY path once Wavr adds a UWB anchor bridge -- flag as future roadmap item, not addable today.

name: NXP Trimension SR150/SR040 UWB Chipset
modality: Automotive/consumer-grade UWB secure ranging chipset (used in car-key and phone UWB)
privacy_note: Chipset-level component, not a consumer end-device; no path into Wavr without custom reference-design hardware and firmware.
notes: Primarily found embedded in car keys / phones (e.g. some Android UWB-capable models); no off-the-shelf standalone module comparable to Qorvo's dev kit.

name: Google Pixel (UWB-capable models, e.g. Pixel 6 Pro+) native UWB
modality: Android UWB ranging via NXP/Qorvo chipset, exposed through Android's UWB API
privacy_note: Android's UWB Ranging API (AOSP `android.uwb`) could theoretically be used by a custom companion app to feed Wavr locally, but no such bridge exists yet -- honestly a roadmap item pending companion-app development.
notes: Closest realistic Android UWB path if Wavr ever builds a companion app; today it is not addable.

name: Generic Eddystone-UID/URL Beacon (assorted Chinese OEM)
modality: Low-cost Eddystone-format BLE beacon for zone/room anchoring
privacy_note: Local broadcast only; no vendor cloud dependency for basic Eddystone frame reception.
notes: ~5-8 EUR; widely available on AliExpress/Amazon; quality/battery life varies by OEM.

name: Raspberry Pi Pico W as BLE scanner/beacon bridge
modality: DIY BLE scanner or beacon node with Wi-Fi backhaul
privacy_note: Fully local; requires custom MicroPython/C firmware to scan BLE and publish over local Wi-Fi/MQTT.
notes: ~6-7 EUR; alternative to ESP32 for admins preferring RPi ecosystem/tooling; needs more DIY firmware work than ESPresense's turnkey ESP32 image.

name: M5Stack AtomS3/ATOM Lite as compact BLE tag or scanner
modality: Compact ESP32-based module usable as either a beacon tag or scanner node
privacy_note: Local only; custom firmware (ESPresense-compatible or custom) required, admin controls exactly what's broadcast/collected.
notes: ~10 EUR; small form factor good for wearable tag use-case; built-in battery options available.

name: BlueCharm BC011/BC021 Bluetooth Beacon
modality: Configurable iBeacon/Eddystone beacon, replaceable battery, IP66 outdoor variants available
privacy_note: Local broadcast only; configuration app is optional and not required for ongoing operation.
notes: ~20 EUR; outdoor-rated variants useful for perimeter/garden zone anchoring alongside indoor rooms.

name: Known smartphone MAC via Wi-Fi ARP (cross-reference with BLE identity)
modality: Wi-Fi network presence of a known phone, correlated with BLE identity mapping for confidence boost
privacy_note: Local ARP/router-table scan only; combining with BLE room-level data improves identity confidence without any new cloud egress.
notes: No new hardware cost (uses existing router/network-scan ingress); pairs naturally with this category's BLE identity mapping for cross-validation.

name: Tapo C210
modality: indoor pan/tilt 2K Wi-Fi camera, person detection
privacy_note: Local RTSP stream available via Tapo app 'Advanced Settings > Camera Account'; frames processed live by Wavr, never persisted or sent to TP-Link cloud if cloud sync disabled.
notes: ~€30-35, Wi-Fi only, indoor, 2K, requires enabling local RTSP + creating camera account

name: Tapo C225
modality: indoor pan/tilt 2K QHD Wi-Fi camera with spotlight, person/pet detection
privacy_note: Local RTSP available; disable cloud storage/notifications for full local-only operation.
notes: ~€35-40, Wi-Fi, indoor, built-in spotlight + siren (siren not exposed via RTSP, only app)

name: Tapo TC40
modality: outdoor 2K pan/tilt Wi-Fi camera, color night vision, person detection
privacy_note: Local RTSP available; weatherproof so keeps camera frames on-prem for perimeter watch, no cloud egress needed for detection.
notes: ~€45-55, Wi-Fi, IP65 outdoor, color night vision, spotlight

name: Tapo C520WS
modality: outdoor 2K pan/tilt Wi-Fi camera with solar option, person detection

notes: ~€60-70, Wi-Fi, IP66, optional solar panel accessory

name: RLC-810A

notes: ~€60-70, PoE, IP66, 4K, well documented ONVIF support

name: RLC-833A
modality: PoE outdoor 4K bullet camera, 5x optical zoom, person/vehicle detection
privacy_note: Local RTSP/ONVIF works fully offline; good option for long-range perimeter without relying on Reolink cloud.

name: E1 Pro
modality: indoor pan/tilt 4K Wi-Fi camera, person/pet detection

name: TrackMix PoE

name: DS-2CD2143G2-I (EasyIP 4MP)

privacy_note: Full local RTSP/ONVIF, common in NVR-free deployments; disable Hik-Connect cloud P2P for zero egress.

name: DS-2CD2385FWD-I (EasyIP 8MP)

name: IPC-HDW2831T-ZS (Lite AI 8MP)

privacy_note: Local ONVIF/RTSP available; keep P2P/cloud (gateway to Dahua servers) disabled for local-only.

name: IPC-HFW2439S-AS-LED-B

privacy_note: Local RTSP/ONVIF; siren/light control local via camera's own relay, no cloud needed.
notes: ~€100-120, PoE, full-color night vision, built-in deterrence; control detail: onboard white-light + siren, controllable via ONVIF/CGI if exposed, else via Dahua app only

name: IP5M-T1179EW

privacy_note: Full local ONVIF/RTSP support, Amcrest cloud (Amcrest View) fully optional, common choice for local-only NVR setups.

name: IP8M-2496EW (4K UltraHD PoE)

name: UniFi Protect G4 Bullet
modality: PoE outdoor 4MP bullet camera, integrated with UniFi Protect NVR AI (person/vehicle)

notes: ~€120-140, PoE, needs UniFi Protect host (NVR/UDM) on same network

name: UniFi Protect G5 Flex

notes: ~€90-100, PoE (or micro-USB adapter), compact form factor

name: UniFi Protect G4 Doorbell Pro
modality: PoE video doorbell, 2K HDR, person/package detection at entry point
privacy_note: Requires local UniFi Protect controller; RTSP can be enabled locally, no cloud dependency for video/detection.
notes: ~€180-200, PoE (may need PoE injector kit), built-in chime relay for real doorbell

name: Wyze Cam v3 (RTSP firmware)
modality: indoor/outdoor 1080p Wi-Fi camera, color night vision, motion detection
privacy_note: Official Wyze 'RTSP Firmware' add-on flashes camera to expose a local RTSP stream, fully bypassing Wyze cloud for video; requires manual firmware flash and loses default Wyze app AI features.
notes: ~€25-35, Wi-Fi, IP65 (v3 outdoor-rated), cheapest local-RTSP option; no onboard person-detection once on RTSP firmware — Wavr's YOLO does that instead

name: Annke C800
modality: PoE outdoor 4K bullet camera, person/vehicle AI detection, color night vision
privacy_note: Local RTSP/ONVIF supported; Annke cloud (Annke Vision) optional only.
notes: ~€70-90, PoE, IP67, 4K, budget-tier alternative to Reolink/Amcrest

name: Annke NC800 (5MP PoE Turret)
modality: PoE outdoor 5MP turret camera with two-way audio, person detection
privacy_note: Local ONVIF/RTSP; two-way audio stream stays on local network via RTSP/ONVIF audio backchannel.
notes: ~€60-80, PoE, IP67, includes mic + speaker

name: R2M
modality: indoor pan/tilt 2K Wi-Fi/PoE camera, person detection
privacy_note: Native ONVIF/RTSP; Foscam Cloud (VIP plan) fully optional, camera works standalone on LAN.
notes: ~€45-55, Wi-Fi + PoE option, indoor, 2K

name: G4P
modality: outdoor 4MP pan/tilt Wi-Fi camera, person/vehicle/pet AI detection, spotlight+siren
privacy_note: Local ONVIF/RTSP supported, camera functions fully without Foscam Cloud.
notes: ~€100-120, Wi-Fi, IP66, 4MP, active deterrence; control detail: onboard siren/spotlight, deterrence controllable mainly via Foscam app; ONVIF alarm-out support varies by firmware

name: Generic 4MP ONVIF PoE Dome Camera
modality: PoE indoor/outdoor dome camera, basic motion detection, generic ONVIF Profile S/T
privacy_note: No-name ONVIF cameras (AliExpress/Amazon generic brands) are typically fully local with no cloud account at all — good for privacy but verify firmware doesn't phone home; audit before trusting.
notes: ~€25-45, PoE, IP66, quality/support varies widely; person detection relies on Wavr's own YOLO, not onboard AI

name: Generic 4G/Battery Solar ONVIF Camera
modality: battery/solar outdoor camera with cellular 4G backhaul, motion-triggered clips
privacy_note: Most cellular/battery cams route video through a manufacturer cloud app with no local RTSP — cloud-only by design; would need a local-RTSP-capable model to fit Wavr's no-egress rule. Flagged roadmap until a verified local-stream battery cam is chosen.
notes: Useful for off-grid perimeter points (gates, driveways) but privacy caveat is real — do not add without confirming local RTSP support

name: eufyCam 3
modality: wire-free battery/solar outdoor camera, on-device person/pet/vehicle AI, local HomeBase storage
privacy_note: Eufy stores locally on HomeBase (good) but does not expose an open RTSP/ONVIF stream to third parties out of the box; would need reverse-engineered integration (e.g. via eufy-security-ws bridge) to reach Wavr — tag honestly as roadmap/needs-bridge, not addable-now.
notes: ~€180-220 per cam + HomeBase required; strong native privacy story but no native Wavr ingress path yet

name: DS-2CD2347G2-LU (ColorVu 4MP Turret)
modality: PoE outdoor 4MP full-color-24/7 turret camera, person/vehicle AcuSense, built-in mic
privacy_note: Local ONVIF/RTSP + audio; ColorVu full-color night vision improves person ID at night for Wavr's own detector without any cloud step.
notes: ~€100-130, PoE, IP67, ColorVu full-color low-light

name: Duo 2 PoE
modality: PoE outdoor dual-lens 8MP wide-angle panoramic camera, person/vehicle detection
privacy_note: Local RTSP/ONVIF; wide panoramic FoV covers more perimeter per camera, fully local.
notes: ~€150-170, PoE, IP66, 180-degree stitched panoramic view

name: SD1A404XB-GNR (4MP Mini PTZ)
modality: PoE outdoor 4MP PTZ (pan-tilt-zoom) camera with auto-tracking, person/vehicle detection
privacy_note: Local ONVIF/RTSP includes PTZ control commands (ONVIF PTZ service) for local pan/tilt/zoom automation with no cloud step.
notes: ~€150-200, PoE, motorized PTZ, ONVIF PTZ profile supported for programmatic control

name: Annke Doorbell Lite

notes: ~€60-80, Wi-Fi/battery, entry-point coverage; confirm RTSP support per SKU/firmware before relying on it

privacy_note: Local via Zigbee2MQTT/HA bridge; no camera, no cloud needed once flashed/paired locally.

name: Temperature and Humidity Sensor T1

name: SNZB-02P Temperature and Humidity Sensor

name: ESP32 Sound Level / Mic Module (INMP441 or SPM1423)

name: Shelly Uni (dry-contact input for 3rd-party glass-break detector)

name: Generic I2S MEMS Mic Decibel Adapter (ESPHome)
modality: converts raw ambient audio to a local dB/event level only, bridges to Wavr as a sound-level sensor
privacy_note: audio is processed on-device into a dB value only; raw waveform is never transmitted or stored — required design to keep mic sensors privacy-safe
notes: ~$10-20 DIY BOM, ESPHome microphone platform, good for baby-cry / loud-argument / glass-break context signals without recording

name: 4-in-1 Motion/Temp/Light/Humidity Sensor ZSE40
modality: PIR motion + temperature + light + humidity combo
privacy_note: local via Z-Wave stick + HA, no cloud dependency
notes: ~$40, Z-Wave, adjustable sensitivity, useful multi-signal context sensor

name: ESP32 DevKitC (WROOM-32)
modality: general-purpose Wi-Fi/BLE MCU host for sensors/relays
privacy_note: Runs custom local firmware; talks to Wavr over LAN MQTT or serial, no cloud required.
notes: ~$6-9; base board for most DIY sensor nodes; dual-core, Wi-Fi+BLE

name: ESP32-S3-DevKitC-1
modality: Wi-Fi/BLE MCU with AI acceleration (vector instructions) for camera/audio nodes
privacy_note: Local firmware only; suited for on-device inference so raw audio/frames need not leave the node.
notes: ~$10-13; more RAM/PSRAM than classic ESP32, USB-OTG, good for cam/mic nodes

name: ESP32-C3-DevKitM-1
modality: low-cost RISC-V Wi-Fi/BLE MCU for simple sensor nodes
privacy_note: Local-only firmware; ideal for cheap single-purpose contact/environmental nodes.
notes: ~$4-6; single-core RISC-V, smaller footprint, good for battery nodes

name: ESP32-CAM (AI-Thinker)
modality: low-cost Wi-Fi camera module (OV2640), can run local motion/person detect
privacy_note: Needs custom firmware exposing local RTSP/HTTP MJPEG stream; frames stay on LAN, never persisted by Wavr, no cloud.
notes: ~$8-10; OV2640 2MP, no audio, PoE not native, needs USB-serial to flash

name: Raspberry Pi 4 Model B (4/8GB)
modality: SBC host for RTSP restreaming, USB radar/BLE dongles, local compute
privacy_note: Runs fully local services (e.g. go2rtc/mediamtx, frigate-style pipelines); no cloud egress if configured local-only.
notes: ~$55-90 board only; hosts USB mmWave/BLE dongles or acts as extra RTSP/ONVIF bridge node

name: Raspberry Pi 5 (4/8GB)
modality: faster SBC host for local YOLO inference, multi-camera restreaming, radar host
privacy_note: Local compute node; can pair with Coral/AI HAT for on-device inference, no frames leave LAN.
notes: ~$60-100 board only; PCIe lane enables NVMe + faster USB for multiple camera streams

name: Raspberry Pi Zero 2 W
modality: compact low-power SBC for single-camera RTSP node or BLE/serial bridge
privacy_note: Local-only bridge node; no cloud services required.
notes: ~$15-18; good for tucking a Pi Camera + local RTSP server into a small enclosure

name: CP2102 USB-to-UART Adapter
modality: USB-serial bridge for flashing/talking to ESP32 or serial mmWave radar
privacy_note: Passive data bridge; carries only local serial traffic, no network exposure by itself.
notes: ~$3-6; most common USB-UART chipset, native driver support on Win/Mac/Linux

name: CH340/CH341 USB-to-UART Adapter
modality: budget USB-serial bridge for ESP32/Arduino flashing and serial radar hosts
privacy_note: Passive local serial bridge; no cloud, no data retention.
notes: ~$1-3; cheapest option, may need extra driver on Windows/older macOS

name: FTDI FT232RL USB-to-UART Adapter
modality: reliable USB-serial bridge, industrial-grade, for radar/ESP hosts
privacy_note: Passive local serial bridge; no cloud dependency.
notes: ~$8-15 (genuine); most stable driver support, avoid counterfeit chips

name: Seeed Studio XIAO ESP32-C3
modality: thumb-sized Wi-Fi/BLE MCU, common host for mmWave/PIR breakout modules
privacy_note: Local custom firmware; no cloud unless user adds it.
notes: ~$5; very small form factor, popular host for Seeed mmWave kits and LD2410

name: M5Stack Core2 (ESP32 + display)
modality: ESP32 dev board with touchscreen, mic, IMU — DIY control panel / sensor hub
privacy_note: Local firmware; can double as an in-home Wavr status/control panel without cloud round-trip.
notes: ~$40-50; touchscreen useful for a local wall-mounted Wavr control panel

name: PoE Splitter + Injector Kit (802.3af/at)
modality: power delivery for PoE IP cameras/ESP32-CAM PoE hats over single Ethernet run
privacy_note: Pure power infrastructure; no data path of its own, enables tidy local wiring for cameras.
notes: ~$15-25 per pair; simplifies running a single cable to ceiling-mounted RTSP cameras

name: External 2.4GHz Wi-Fi/BLE Antenna (RP-SMA, 5-9dBi)
modality: range-extension antenna for ESP32/BLE nodes and USB Wi-Fi adapters
privacy_note: Passive RF component; no data handling, purely improves signal range for existing ingress paths.
notes: ~$5-12; improves BLE/ESPresense reliability in far rooms or through walls

name: USB Wi-Fi Adapter, AC1300 dual-band (RTL8812AU/MT7612U)
modality: adds/extends Wi-Fi connectivity to Pi/PC hosts running Wavr services
privacy_note: Pure network infrastructure; carries whatever ingress path (RTSP/MQTT/BLE) is already local.
notes: ~$15-25; useful when host box lacks reliable onboard Wi-Fi or needs monitor mode for network-scan

name: USB Bluetooth 4.0/5.0 Dongle (CSR/Realtek chipset)
modality: adds BLE scanning capability to a Pi/PC host for advertisement-based presence
privacy_note: Passive advertisement scanning only; local host processes RSSI, no cloud round-trip.
notes: ~$8-15; lets a headless Pi/PC act as a BLE presence scanner without native onboard BT

name: Coral USB Accelerator (Edge TPU)
modality: USB ML accelerator to speed up local YOLO/person-detection inference
privacy_note: Pure local inference accelerator; frames processed on-device, nothing leaves the box, no cloud.
notes: ~$60-75; offloads YOLO/pose inference from CPU on Pi/PC hosts, big FPS/latency win

name: M.2 NVMe-to-USB3.2 Enclosure/Adapter
modality: fast local storage for Wavr host (clip buffers, model files, logs)
privacy_note: Storage-only component; keeps any transient buffers strictly local, no network path.
notes: ~$15-25; useful for Pi/mini-PC hosts needing fast local storage for multi-camera pipelines

name: Olimex ESP32-POE
modality: ESP32 dev board with native 802.3af PoE, Ethernet, for wired reliable sensor/relay nodes
privacy_note: Local custom firmware over wired Ethernet; avoids Wi-Fi congestion, no cloud required.
notes: ~$25-30; good for fixed sensor/relay nodes needing power+data on one cable

name: ESP-Prog USB JTAG/UART Programmer
modality: dedicated flashing/debugging adapter for ESP32 family boards
privacy_note: Dev/flashing tool only; not part of runtime data path.
notes: ~$15-18; speeds up flashing/debugging multiple ESP nodes during DIY build-out

name: Waveshare ESP32-S3-ETH (with PoE option)
modality: ESP32-S3 board with onboard Ethernet PHY, good for wired reliable camera/relay hub
privacy_note: Local firmware over wired Ethernet; deterministic LAN path, no cloud.
notes: ~$15-20; alternative to Olimex, more RAM/PSRAM for on-device inference tasks

name: ZBDongle-E
modality: Zigbee 3.0 USB coordinator (EFR32/Silicon Labs), feeds Zigbee2MQTT
privacy_note: Local Zigbee radio; runs through local Zigbee2MQTT + local MQTT broker, no cloud egress
notes: ~$20-25, USB, requires Zigbee2MQTT (Docker/HA add-on) + MQTT broker; most flexible/most documented Zigbee coordinator

name: ZBDongle-P
modality: Zigbee 3.0 USB coordinator (TI CC2652P), higher power/range, feeds Zigbee2MQTT
privacy_note: Local Zigbee radio; local Zigbee2MQTT + MQTT, no cloud
notes: ~$25-30, USB, better range/PA+LNA than -E, popular for larger homes

name: ConBee II
modality: Zigbee USB coordinator, feeds deCONZ or Zigbee2MQTT
privacy_note: Local; deCONZ/Zigbee2MQTT can bridge to local MQTT, no cloud dependency
notes: ~$45, USB, mature firmware, wide device compatibility

name: SkyConnect
modality: Zigbee + Thread combo USB radio for Home Assistant
privacy_note: Local radio operated by HA OS/ZHA/OTBR; Wavr consumes via local HA API, no cloud
notes: ~$40, USB, official HA hardware, ZHA or Zigbee2MQTT + OpenThread Border Router

name: Z-Stick 7
modality: Z-Wave 700-series USB controller, feeds Z-Wave JS (UI)
privacy_note: Local Z-Wave mesh; Z-Wave JS UI can publish to local MQTT, no cloud egress
notes: ~$60-70, USB, S2 security, needs Z-Wave JS/Z-Wave JS UI server

name: ZST39 800LR
modality: Z-Wave 800-series long-range USB controller
privacy_note: Local Z-Wave LR mesh via Z-Wave JS UI to local MQTT, no cloud
notes: ~$50, extended range 800-series chip, good for large properties

name: Home Assistant Yellow
modality: Full local smart-home hub (HA OS) with built-in Zigbee/Thread radio, PoE option
privacy_note: Runs fully local (HA OS); Wavr integrates via HA local REST/WebSocket API, no cloud required
notes: ~$150-200, built-in NVMe option, aggregates hundreds of integrations into one HA instance

name: Home Assistant Green
modality: Entry-level local smart-home hub appliance (HA OS)
privacy_note: Local HA OS box; Wavr talks local API, no cloud dependency
notes: ~$100, no radio built in (pair with SkyConnect/dongle), fanless, low power

name: Elevation C-8
modality: Local Zigbee+Z-Wave hub with on-device automation engine
privacy_note: Processing fully local on hub; MQTT app/community integration bridges to Wavr with no cloud round-trip
notes: ~$160, Zigbee+Z-Wave radios onboard, MakerAPI + MQTT link community apps

name: Shelly Plus 2PM
modality: Gen2 dual-relay WiFi switch/roller controller, local MQTT
privacy_note: Local MQTT/RPC, cloud can be fully disabled in device settings
notes: ~$25, WiFi, supports roller/shutter mode + power metering per channel

name: Shelly H&T Gen3
modality: Battery WiFi temperature + humidity sensor, local MQTT
privacy_note: Local MQTT publish, no cloud required for readings
notes: ~$25, battery ~1yr, WiFi (higher battery drain than Zigbee equivalents)

name: BASICR4
modality: inline WiFi relay/switch module for appliances, garage, gate motors
privacy_note: Stock firmware is cloud (eWeLink); official DIY mode exposes a local HTTP API but guaranteed local-only operation requires a Tasmota/ESPHome reflash.
notes: ~$8-12, WiFi, bare relay module, easy ESPHome/Tasmota reflash; common building block for garage/gate/appliance control.

name: Tasmota (generic ESP8266/ESP32 firmware)
modality: Open-source firmware turning ESP-based smart plugs/relays/sensors into local MQTT devices
privacy_note: Fully local MQTT by design, no vendor cloud at all once flashed
notes: free, flash via Tasmotizer/web-installer, huge device compatibility list (Sonoff, Athom, generic plugs)

name: ESPHome (generic ESP32/ESP8266 firmware)
modality: YAML-defined firmware exposing any GPIO/I2C/UART sensor or relay via native API or MQTT, deep HA integration
privacy_note: Config-driven local firmware; publishes to local MQTT broker or native API only, no cloud dependency.
notes: free; cost = board + sensor (~$5-15); most flexible way to add commodity PIR/door/temp/BLE-tracker sensors to Wavr; compiles per-device firmware.

name: Athom Smart Plug v2 (Tasmota/ESPHome preflashed)
modality: ESP32 WiFi smart plug shipped with open firmware, local MQTT, power metering
privacy_note: Ships open-firmware, local-only MQTT out of the box, no forced cloud
notes: ~$15-18, WiFi, energy monitoring, popular with ESPHome/Tasmota community

name: Mosquitto
modality: Open-source local MQTT broker — the backbone all MQTT devices/bridges publish into
privacy_note: Self-hosted on the same box/LAN as Wavr; zero external egress by design
notes: free, runs as Docker container or HA add-on, TLS+auth recommended, foundation of the MQTT ingress path

name: Zigbee2MQTT
modality: Software bridge translating Zigbee coordinator traffic into local MQTT topics for 4000+ devices
privacy_note: Runs fully local (Docker/HA add-on), publishes only to local Mosquitto, no cloud dependency
notes: free, requires a supported Zigbee coordinator (ZBDongle-E/P, ConBee II, etc.), largest Zigbee device compatibility DB

name: Z-Wave JS UI
modality: Software bridge exposing Z-Wave network state/control over local MQTT and REST
privacy_note: Self-hosted Docker/HA add-on, local MQTT only, no cloud
notes: free, pairs with any 500/700/800-series Z-Wave USB stick, web UI for pairing/management

name: Home Assistant Core/OS
modality: Central local automation platform aggregating Zigbee/Z-Wave/WiFi/BLE/MQTT integrations under one API
privacy_note: Runs on user's own hardware (RPi/NUC/VM), local REST/WebSocket API for Wavr, Nabu Casa cloud is opt-in only
notes: free (self-hosted), single richest integration surface (2000+), recommended default bridge for Wavr's HA ingress

name: MQTT/HA discovery generic bridge template
modality: Any third-party integration publishing HA MQTT-discovery-compliant sensors/switches
privacy_note: Depends on the specific integration; discovery payloads stay on local broker, Wavr never needs external calls
notes: free, covers custom scripts/integrations that follow HA's MQTT discovery spec, catch-all long-tail entry

name: Hub M2
modality: Zigbee hub with local LAN API + BLE gateway function, also HomeKit bridge
privacy_note: Stock app requires Aqara cloud account for setup; local LAN API/HA integration avoids ongoing cloud traffic but initial pairing depends on Aqara cloud
notes: ~$45, Zigbee 3.0 + BLE, requires HA Aqara integration for local-first operation, IR blaster onboard

name: DIRIGERA
modality: Zigbee hub for TRÅDFRI/Zigbee 3.0 devices, local API supported via HA integration
privacy_note: Has a documented local API (token-based); HA integration keeps traffic on-LAN, no per-event cloud round trip
notes: ~$70, Zigbee 3.0 + Matter support, best for IKEA smart bulbs/blinds fleets

name: Tapo H200
modality: WiFi hub for Tapo Zigbee/RF sensors (contact, motion, water leak)
privacy_note: Cloud-only architecture (Tapo app/account required, no documented local API); would need reverse-engineered or future official local bridge
notes: ~$25, budget option, currently NOT recommended for Wavr due to cloud-only ingress

name: NSPanel Pro
modality: Zigbee wall panel hub with touchscreen, local Home Assistant integration (via eWeLink or flashed HA add-on)
privacy_note: Supports local HA add-on mode (NSPanel Pro Manager) avoiding eWeLink cloud; caveat if left on stock cloud firmware
notes: ~$120, built-in Zigbee coordinator + touchscreen UI, dual-use as hub + control panel

name: Generic industrial MQTT gateway (Modbus/RS485-to-MQTT)
modality: Modbus/RS485 industrial sensor bus bridged to local MQTT for env/utility sensors
privacy_note: Local serial-to-MQTT bridge, no cloud; useful for advanced users wiring HVAC/water/energy metering into Wavr
notes: varies $30-100, niche/DIY, relevant for whole-home utility monitoring integration

name: Generic Zigbee mains-powered repeater/plug
modality: Zigbee router/repeater device (mains smart plug) extending mesh range for Zigbee2MQTT/HA ZHA networks
privacy_note: Local Zigbee mesh extension only, no data of its own beyond relay + optional plug telemetry, no cloud
notes: ~$15-20, any Zigbee mains plug acts as a repeater — recommended for extending coverage in larger homes

name: HLK-LD2410B
modality: 24GHz FMCW presence + motion detection with static/moving distance gates
privacy_note: Local UART module, no radio egress at all — no camera, no cloud path possible.
notes: ~$5-8 bare module, 3.3V UART, 9 configurable range gates, common in DIY presence sensors; needs USB-serial or MCU to reach Wavr

name: HLK-LD2410C
modality: 24GHz FMCW presence + motion, improved firmware/AT-command set over LD2410B
privacy_note: Local UART only, no wireless radio on the module itself.
notes: ~$6-9, pin-compatible with LD2410B, widely used in ESPHome community presence boards

name: LD2410 + ESP32 mmWave Presence Node (DIY)
modality: 24GHz mmWave presence bridged to Wi-Fi/MQTT via ESPHome or custom firmware
privacy_note: Local WiFi/MQTT only if configured with local API (no cloud); requires ESPHome build/flash to keep fully local.
notes: ~$8-15 all-in (LD2410/LD2410C module + ESP32 dev board), common AliExpress combo boards, very popular ESPHome pairing; needs custom firmware flash to expose over MQTT/native API.

name: HLK-LD2412
modality: 24GHz FMCW presence, next-gen anti-interference vs LD2410 series
privacy_note: Local UART only, same trust model as LD2410 family.
notes: ~$7-10, newer gen with better false-trigger rejection near curtains/fans, drop-in UART protocol similar to LD2410

name: HLK-LD2450
modality: 24GHz multi-target tracking radar, outputs X/Y coordinates + speed for up to 3 targets
privacy_note: Coordinate-only output over local UART, no imagery, no cloud dependency.
notes: ~$10-14, 360-degree or fan FOV depending on antenna variant, popular for room-zone occupancy grids

name: HLK-LD1125H
modality: 24GHz single-target motion + presence detection, simple binary/serial output
privacy_note: Local UART, no wireless stack on the module.
notes: ~$5-7, cheaper/simpler than LD2410, coarser range resolution, decent for closet/small-room presence

name: HLK-LD1115H
modality: 24GHz motion + presence radar, predecessor to LD1125H with dual-channel output
privacy_note: Local UART only.
notes: ~$5-7, older gen, still sold, similar use case to LD1125H

name: DFRobot SEN0395 mmWave Human Presence Sensor
modality: 24GHz FMCW presence detection with configurable detection zone/sensitivity via UART/GPIO
privacy_note: Local UART/GPIO module, no cloud egress.
notes: ~$13-16, Gravity-format breakout, easier hobbyist wiring than bare Hi-Link modules, also has digital output pin

name: DFRobot C4001 mmWave Radar (SEN0592)
modality: 24GHz FMCW presence + speed/distance ranging, longer range than SEN0395, UART and I2C output

notes: ~$20-25, up to ~20m range mode available, good for hallway/perimeter-style zone coverage

name: Seeed Studio MR24HPC1 mmWave Human Presence Sensor
modality: 24GHz FMCW radar, human static/moving presence with body-motion parameter output

name: Seeed Studio MR60FDA2 mmWave Fall Detection Sensor

privacy_note: Local UART, fall events should be treated live-only, no imagery captured.
notes: ~$30-40, ceiling or high-wall mount recommended for best fall-angle coverage, aimed at elder-care use cases

name: Everything Presence One
modality: LD2410-based mmWave + PIR + light/temp/humidity combo sensor, open-source ESPHome firmware
privacy_note: Runs ESPHome locally; can be configured for local-only native API/MQTT with no cloud dependency.
notes: ~$45-55 assembled, open hardware/firmware, ceiling or wall mount, exposes distance + PIR + env data over MQTT/native API

name: Everything Presence Lite

name: Aqara Presence Sensor FP2
modality: 60GHz FMCW radar with multi-zone occupancy mapping, fall detection, and multi-person tracking
privacy_note: Native app path touches Aqara cloud; integrate strictly via the local Aqara HA integration (local-only mode) to avoid new cloud egress into Wavr.

name: Aqara Presence Sensor FP1E
modality: mmWave presence detection with single-zone occupancy + motion, successor to original FP1

name: Tuya ZY-M100 mmWave Presence Module (white-label)

name: Generic Tuya/Zigbee mmWave Human Presence Sensor

privacy_note: Cloud-dependent by default via Tuya/Zigbee2MQTT gateway path; run through Zigbee2MQTT + local MQTT broker to keep it local-first.
notes: ~$15-20, quality varies widely by vendor, best paired with Zigbee2MQTT rather than the Tuya app/hub for a fully local path

name: LD2410 USB-Serial Breakout (direct-to-PC)

notes: ~$12-18, simplest possible integration path when the Wavr box is physically near the sensed room

name: Seeed Studio MR24HPC2 mmWave Presence + Fall Sensor
modality: 24GHz radar combining human presence, motion, and basic fall/posture-change detection in one module
privacy_note: Local UART, no imaging, fall flag should be treated live-only.

name: ESP32 mmWave-to-MQTT Gateway (DIY, any LD-series module)

name: HC-SR501 PIR Motion Sensor (via ESP8266/ESP32)

name: HC-SR505 Mini PIR Motion Sensor (via ESP)

name: AM312 Micro PIR Motion Sensor (via ESP)
modality: ultra-compact passive infrared motion detection
privacy_note: Local GPIO digital output only, no imagery or cloud.

name: Aqara Motion Sensor P1

name: Aqara Motion Sensor (RTCGQ11LM, gen 1)

name: Philips Hue Motion Sensor

notes: ~$40, Zigbee (Hue mesh or Z2M), battery (2xAA, ~2yr), also exposes temp + lux, indoor/outdoor variants exist.

name: Philips Hue Outdoor Motion Sensor
modality: weatherproof PIR motion + ambient light + temperature
privacy_note: Local via Hue Bridge/Z2M bridged into HA; no imagery.
notes: ~$45, IP54 weatherproof, Zigbee, battery powered, good for driveway/garden perimeter coverage.

name: Sonoff SNZB-03 Zigbee Motion Sensor
modality: PIR motion detection, Zigbee
privacy_note: Runs fully local once paired to Zigbee2MQTT/HA coordinator; no eWeLink cloud dependency needed in that mode.
notes: ~$10, cheapest reliable Zigbee PIR, CR2450 battery, ~1yr life, well supported in Z2M/ZHA.

name: Tuya Zigbee PIR Motion Sensor
modality: PIR motion + lux, Zigbee
privacy_note: Local through Zigbee2MQTT (Tuya cloud not required once paired to Z2M); avoid Tuya Smart Life app pairing to prevent cloud dependency.
notes: ~$10-15, widely rebadged (various brands), battery powered, decent Z2M support but variable quality across batches.

name: Tuya Wi-Fi PIR Motion Sensor
modality: PIR motion detection, Wi-Fi
privacy_note: Cloud-dependent by default (Tuya cloud); use LocalTuya integration in HA to keep data local, otherwise this device egresses to Tuya cloud — flag caveat.
notes: ~$8-12, battery or USB powered, requires LocalTuya HA integration for local-only operation; skip if avoiding any Tuya cloud pairing step.

name: Sonoff SNZB-03P Zigbee Motion Sensor (gen 2)
modality: PIR motion detection with configurable sensitivity, Zigbee 3.0

notes: ~$12, CR2450 battery ~2yr, configurable sensitivity/keep-time in Z2M, good value upgrade pick.

name: AMG8833 8x8 Thermopile Grid-EYE Sensor (via ESP)
modality: low-res thermal grid presence/occupancy (non-imaging heat blob detection)

name: MLX90640 32x24 Thermal Camera Module (via ESP)

privacy_note: Low-res thermal (768px) still non-identifying (no facial/visual detail); frames processed locally, never persisted, strong option for bedroom/bathroom occupancy without RGB camera privacy concerns.
notes: ~$50-60 module, I2C, wider FoV (55x35°) than AMG8833, custom ESP/firmware parsing needed, can approximate posture/fall via blob shape heuristics (experimental).

name: Generic 360° Ceiling PIR Occupancy Sensor (mains, relay output)
modality: wide-angle ceiling-mount PIR occupancy with dry-contact/relay output
privacy_note: Relay/dry-contact output read by ESP GPIO, fully local, no imagery.
notes: ~$15-30, 360° ceiling mount, 6-8m diameter coverage, common in commercial lighting control, needs ESP to read relay/0-10V output into Wavr.

name: Philips Hue Motion Sensor (2nd gen, temp/lux update)
modality: PIR motion + ambient light + temperature, Zigbee, improved firmware
privacy_note: Local via Hue Bridge (local API supported) or Z2M; disable Hue cloud remote access for strict local-only operation.
notes: ~$40, same form factor as original, improved sensitivity tuning, magnetic swivel mount, indoor use.

name: IKEA TRÅDFRI Motion Sensor
modality: PIR motion detection, Zigbee
privacy_note: Fully local through Zigbee2MQTT/HA, no IKEA cloud (Dirigera) required if paired directly to Z2M coordinator.
notes: ~$12-15, budget Zigbee PIR, 2xAAA battery, ~1-2yr life, well supported in Z2M.

name: IKEA TRÅDFRI Motion Sensor (updated model, wireless motion sensor v2)
modality: PIR motion detection with adjustable detection range, Zigbee
privacy_note: Local via Z2M/HA bridge; no cloud needed.
notes: ~$15, physical range dial (short/medium/long), battery powered, good budget option for hallways.

name: Shelly Motion 2
modality: PIR motion + lux + vibration + temperature, Wi-Fi
privacy_note: Shelly devices expose a local HTTP/CoAP/MQTT API — can run fully local without Shelly Cloud; recommend disabling cloud toggle in device settings.
notes: ~$35-40, Wi-Fi (no hub needed), battery (18650, ~1yr), also has vibration/tamper detection, native MQTT makes direct Wavr ingest possible without HA in some setups.

name: Aeotec TriSensor (motion + temp + lux)
modality: PIR motion + temperature + ambient light, Zigbee
privacy_note: Local via Z2M/HA; no cloud dependency once paired to local Zigbee coordinator.
notes: ~$30, CR2450 battery, compact form factor, 3-in-1 sensing reduces device count.

name: Everspring SP815 Z-Wave PIR Motion Sensor
modality: PIR motion detection, Z-Wave
privacy_note: Local via Z-Wave JS/HA bridge, no cloud dependency; Z-Wave is inherently local mesh.
notes: ~$35-40, Z-Wave Plus, battery powered, good option if household already has a Z-Wave stick/mesh instead of Zigbee.

name: Fibaro Motion Sensor (FGMS-001/FGMS-001 ZW5)
modality: PIR motion + temperature + ambient light + vibration/tamper, Z-Wave
privacy_note: Local via Z-Wave JS/HA, no cloud dependency; well-regarded multi-sensor for privacy-conscious local setups.
notes: ~$45-55, CR123A battery, RGB LED status ring, configurable sensitivity/blind time, premium option.

name: SONOFF Zigbee Motion Sensor SNZB-03 (Basic/older stock)
modality: PIR motion detection, Zigbee (basic feature set)
privacy_note: Local via Z2M/HA; identical privacy profile to SNZB-03 line.
notes: ~$10, same hardware family as SNZB-03, older firmware batches sometimes need manual Z2M quirk override.

name: Bosch Smart Home Motion Detector II
modality: PIR motion + ambient light, proprietary mesh (bridgeable)
privacy_note: Requires Bosch Smart Home Controller bridged into HA; check whether local push API is available on your controller firmware version, else data transits Bosch cloud — caveat.
notes: ~$50, battery powered, needs Bosch hub, best for households already invested in Bosch ecosystem.

name: ESP32-CAM + External PIR Trigger (hybrid wake-on-motion module)
modality: PIR-gated motion trigger for low-power wake/snapshot workflows
privacy_note: PIR-only signal used for Wavr presence event; if the onboard camera is also used, frames must never be persisted per Wavr camera policy — treat as PIR entry, camera use is out of scope here.
notes: ~$8-10 combo board, popular DIY hack for battery-friendly triggered sensing, requires custom ESP firmware; do not enable frame capture without following camera privacy invariant.

name: Lidl Silvercrest Zigbee Motion Sensor (SMSZ 1A1)
modality: PIR motion detection, Zigbee (Tuya-based OEM)
privacy_note: Local via Z2M/HA, Tuya-based Zigbee radio but no cloud requirement once paired locally.
notes: ~$8-12 (seasonal Lidl stock), CR2450 battery, budget European option, decent Z2M support.

name: Wemos D1 Mini + HC-SR501 DIY PIR Node
modality: DIY Wi-Fi PIR motion node built on ESP8266 dev board
privacy_note: Fully local Wi-Fi MQTT publish direct to Wavr broker, no cloud, DIY firmware fully auditable.
notes: ~$5-8 total parts, most common DIY PIR-to-Wavr pattern, publishes MQTT topic on GPIO interrupt, easy to batch across many rooms cheaply.

name: OpenWrt Router (ARP/DHCP presence)
modality: device presence via ARP table + DHCP lease scanning
privacy_note: fully local; Wavr polls router API/SSH over LAN, no cloud egress
notes: any router flashed w/ OpenWrt; free; needs SSH or ubus JSON-RPC access

name: UniFi Dream Machine / UDM-Pro
modality: device presence + client list via UniFi Network API
privacy_note: local controller API on LAN; no cloud calls needed if local-only mode used
notes: ~€180-400; local API key; also gives per-AP RSSI for rough room estimate

name: UniFi Access Point (RSSI-based room estimate)
modality: connected-client RSSI per AP, used for coarse room localization
privacy_note: local controller data only; identifies known device MACs, not people directly
notes: ~€90-200/AP; accuracy is per-AP not per-room unless dense mesh

name: a proprietary scanner Agent (desktop/NAS network scanner)
modality: continuous LAN device discovery + presence via ARP/mDNS
privacy_note: free agent runs locally (e.g. on a NAS/RPi); a proprietary scanner cloud sync is optional and should stay OFF for Wavr use
notes: free tier sufficient; run on always-on box (RPi, NAS, mini PC)

name: Raspberry Pi + nmap/arp-scan cron job
modality: periodic ARP/nmap sweep of subnet for known-MAC presence
privacy_note: fully local script, no third-party service, results only touch Wavr box
notes: RPi Zero 2W ~€20; DIY; needs same L2 subnet as targets

name: ESP32-CSI-Tool (Wi-Fi CSI presence sensing)
modality: channel state info amplitude/phase variance for motion/presence, no wearable needed
privacy_note: would be fully local (raw CSI never needs to leave the box) once integrated; today Wavr has no native CSI decoder/pipeline
notes: ESP32 dev board ~€6-10 x2 (TX+RX); open-source firmware exists but needs custom Wavr-side ML pipeline to be usable

name: ESP32 CSI single-AP fall/breathing research rig
modality: experimental CSI-based breathing/fall detection (research-grade)
privacy_note: would stay fully local; not yet in Wavr — flagged as future R&D, accuracy unproven outside lab papers
notes: very DIY, unstable across firmware versions/antennas; treat as experimental only

name: Nexmon CSI on Raspberry Pi 4B (BCM43455c0)
modality: monitor-mode CSI extraction from onboard Broadcom Wi-Fi chip
privacy_note: processing would be fully on-device/local; not yet wired into Wavr ingestion
notes: RPi 4B ~€45-60; nexmon patch fragile across kernel updates; needs a Wavr CSI adapter to be usable

name: Nexmon CSI on OpenWrt router (TP-Link Archer C7)
modality: router-side CSI extraction for whole-home motion sensing (no extra hardware)
privacy_note: would be local-only (CSI stays on router/LAN); no Wavr decoder pipeline exists today
notes: ~€60-90 router; well-documented nexmon target but router-grade CPU limits sample rate

name: Alfa AWUS036AXML (Wi-Fi 6E monitor-mode USB adapter)
modality: USB monitor-mode adapter for passive Wi-Fi frame capture (probe requests, device fingerprinting)
privacy_note: would be fully local passive sniffing; Wavr has no monitor-mode probe-request ingestion pipeline yet
notes: ~€45-60; MediaTek MT7921AU chipset; Linux monitor mode supported natively

name: Alfa AWUS036ACHM (dual-band monitor-mode USB adapter)
modality: long-range USB Wi-Fi adapter usable in monitor mode for passive presence sniffing
privacy_note: local passive capture only, no cloud; awaiting Wavr probe-request pipeline
notes: ~€35-45; Realtek RTL8812AU chipset; popular pentest adapter, high-gain antenna

name: Panda PAU09 N600 Dual-Band USB Adapter
modality: budget USB monitor-mode-capable adapter for passive Wi-Fi scanning
privacy_note: local only if/when integrated; no current Wavr ingestion path
notes: ~€25-35; RTL8814AU chipset; commonly used in Kali/Wi-Fi pentest kits

name: TP-Link Deco Mesh Node (per-node client list)
modality: mesh AP exposing per-node connected-client list as a coarse room/zone signal
privacy_note: local app/API pull of client table over LAN; Deco cloud account optional and not required for local query
notes: ~€60-100/node; API access sometimes needs local reverse-engineered endpoint or TP-Link Deco app token

name: ASUS AiMesh Node
modality: mesh Wi-Fi node with client-list JSON API for per-node device presence
privacy_note: local HTTP API on router firmware, no vendor cloud dependency
notes: ~€80-150/node; ASUSWRT/Merlin firmware exposes richer local API

name: Amazon eero Mesh Node
modality: mesh node; device presence only reachable through eero's cloud API
privacy_note: CAVEAT: no documented local API — client list requires eero cloud account, which conflicts with Wavr's no-new-cloud-egress rule; not addable without accepting that tradeoff
notes: ~€100-140/node; closed ecosystem, avoid unless user explicitly accepts cloud dependency

name: Google Nest Wifi Pro
modality: mesh node; client presence only via Google Home cloud account
privacy_note: CAVEAT: cloud-only management, no local device API — violates local-first/no-new-cloud-egress invariant, flagged not recommended
notes: ~€130-200/node; closed firmware; would need HA bridge (unofficial) to get any local signal

name: GL.iNet Travel/Mini Router (OpenWrt-based)
modality: compact OpenWrt router with SSH/API access for ARP-based presence, usable as dedicated sensing AP per room
privacy_note: fully local; ships with OpenWrt/LuCI, SSH enabled by default, no cloud needed
notes: ~€25-50; good low-cost dedicated per-room AP for room-level ARP presence

name: ESP32 Wi-Fi Probe-Request Sniffer (DIY, promiscuous mode)
modality: ESP32 in promiscuous mode logging probe-request frames + RSSI to estimate nearby phones/devices
privacy_note: custom firmware keeps MAC hashing/processing local; sends only presence events over Wi-Fi/MQTT to Wavr, no cloud
notes: ~€6-10 ESP32 board; DIY firmware required (open reference sketches exist); MAC randomization on modern phones limits reliability

name: ESP32 ARP-to-MQTT Presence Bridge (DIY)
modality: ESP32 pings/ARPs a list of known-MAC devices per subnet segment and republishes presence via MQTT
privacy_note: runs fully on LAN, publishes to local MQTT broker only, no vendor cloud
notes: ~€6-10; simple Arduino/ESP-IDF sketch; good for segmented VLANs where router-level scan can't see everything

name: Netgear Orbi Mesh Satellite
modality: mesh satellite exposing local web UI client list per node for zone-level presence
privacy_note: local admin UI/API scrape over LAN; Orbi cloud account not required for basic client list
notes: ~€90-180/satellite; local API undocumented but scrapeable via router web UI

name: TP-Link Powerline Adapter (network backhaul, non-sensing)
modality: powerline Ethernet-over-mains backhaul to extend LAN reach to a sensing AP/hub in another room
privacy_note: purely a network transport accessory, no sensing/detection itself; included as infra to extend coverage of other ingress nodes
notes: ~€40-70/pair; useful for reaching a PoE camera/hub where Wi-Fi/Ethernet is weak; not a sensor, an enabler

name: Ubiquiti PoE Switch (network infra for hub/cams)
modality: PoE switch powering/backhauling RTSP cameras, APs, and ESP bridges on the local network
privacy_note: infrastructure only, no detection; enables reliable local wiring for other Wavr ingress devices
notes: ~€100-250 depending on port count; recommended backbone for a serious multi-camera/adapter Wavr install

name: Wemos D1 Mini (ESP8266) Probe/Beacon Scanner (DIY)
modality: cheap ESP8266 board sniffing Wi-Fi beacon/probe frames for coarse device-presence heuristics
privacy_note: custom firmware, local MQTT publish only, no cloud dependency
notes: ~€3-5; ESP8266 has weaker promiscuous-mode API than ESP32, less reliable but very cheap

name: Linksys Velop Mesh Node
modality: mesh node; local device list available via LAN JSON-RPC on some firmware versions
privacy_note: local JNAP API over LAN (no auth token needed on same subnet in most firmware); avoids Linksys cloud app
notes: ~€70-140/node; JNAP API is unofficial but widely documented by community

name: Raspberry Pi + external monitor-mode dongle (hostapd/tcpdump rig)
modality: dedicated always-on box combining a monitor-mode USB adapter with tcpdump/Kismet for passive scanning, feeding Wavr
privacy_note: fully local processing planned; Wavr lacks a Kismet/tcpdump ingestion adapter today
notes: RPi ~€40 + Alfa/Panda dongle ~€30-60; good DIY passive-scan node once pipeline exists
